Submodules

news.admin module

admin.py - Django admin site configuration for the News Application.

Registers all models with custom admin classes to improve usability in the admin panel.

class news.admin.ApprovedArticleLogAdmin(model, admin_site)

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for ApprovedArticleLog (read-only).

has_add_permission(request)

Return True if the given request has permission to add an object. Can be overridden by the user in subclasses.

has_change_permission(request, obj=None)

Return True if the given request has permission to change the given Django model instance, the default implementation doesn’t examine the obj parameter.

Can be overridden by the user in subclasses. In such case it should return True if the given request has permission to change the obj model instance. If obj is None, this should return True if the given request has permission to change any object of the given type.

list_display = ('article', 'logged_at', 'notes')
property media
readonly_fields = ('article', 'logged_at', 'notes')
class news.admin.ArticleAdmin(model, admin_site)

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin configuration for Article.

actions = ['approve_articles']
approve_articles(request, queryset)

Bulk-approve selected articles from the admin panel.

date_hierarchy = 'created_at'
filter_horizontal = ('tags',)
list_display = ('title', 'author', 'publisher', 'status', 'approved', 'created_at')
list_filter = ('status', 'approved', 'publisher', 'section')
property media
readonly_fields = ('approved_at', 'approved_by')
search_fields = ('title', 'author__username')
class news.admin.CustomUserAdmin(model, admin_site)

Bases: UserAdmin

Admin configuration for the custom user model.

add_fieldsets = ((None, {'classes': ('wide',), 'fields': ('username', 'usable_password', 'password1', 'password2')}), ('News App Role', {'fields': ('role',)}))
fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), ('News App Role', {'fields': ('role', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists')}))
list_display = ('username', 'email', 'role', 'is_staff', 'is_active')
list_filter = ('role', 'is_staff', 'is_active', 'groups')
property media
search_fields = ('username', 'email', 'first_name', 'last_name')

news.apps module

apps.py - Application configuration for the ‘news’ app.

Connects Django signals when the application is ready so that article approval events trigger subscriber notifications and API logging.

class news.apps.NewsConfig(app_name, app_module)

Bases: AppConfig

default_auto_field = 'django.db.models.BigAutoField'
name = 'news'
ready()

Import signals module to register all signal handlers. Groups are created via the post_migrate signal to avoid accessing the database during app initialisation (which causes a RuntimeWarning).

news.forms module

forms.py - Django forms for the News Application.

Covers user registration, article creation/editing, newsletter creation/editing, and publisher management.

class news.forms.ArticleForm(*args, **kwargs)

Bases: ModelForm

Form for journalists to create or edit an article.

Fields

titlestr

Title of the article.

sectionstr

Section/category of the article.

tagsQuerySet[Tag]

Tags for the article.

publisherPublisher or None

Publisher for the article (optional).

weather_locationstr

Location for weather articles.

story_imageImage

Optional image for the article.

contentstr

Main content of the article.

auto_weather_updatebool

Whether to auto-generate a weather summary (for Weather section).

Bases: object

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class news.forms.NewsletterForm(*args, **kwargs)

Bases: ModelForm

Form for journalists/editors to create or edit a newsletter.

Fields

titlestr

Title of the newsletter.

descriptionstr

Description of the newsletter.

articlesQuerySet[Article]

Articles included in the newsletter (only approved articles).

Bases: object

fields = ['title', 'description', 'articles']

alias of Newsletter

class news.forms.PublisherForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)

class news.forms.RegistrationForm(*args, **kwargs)

Bases: UserCreationForm

Registration form that lets a new user choose their role (Reader or Journalist).

Editors can only be created through the admin panel to prevent privilege escalation.

Fields

usernamestr

The username for the new user.

emailstr

The user’s email address.

rolestr

The user’s role (Reader or Journalist).

password1, password2str

Password fields.

ALLOWED_ROLES = [(Role.READER, 'Reader'), (Role.JOURNALIST, 'Journalist')]
class Meta

Bases: object

fields = ['username', 'email', 'role', 'password1', 'password2']
model

alias of CustomUser

base_fields = {'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.ChoiceField object>, 'username': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}
declared_fields = {'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.ChoiceField object>}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

save(commit=True)

Save the user and assign them to the appropriate group.

Parameters

commitbool, optional

Whether to commit the save to the database. Defaults to True.

Returns

CustomUser

The created user instance.

Bases: ModelForm

Form that lets a Reader manage their publisher and journalist subscriptions.

Publishers the reader is subscribed to.

subscribed_journalistsQuerySet[CustomUser]

news.models module

models.py - Data models for the News Application.

Defines CustomUser, Publisher, Article, Newsletter, and ApprovedArticleLog (used by the REST API to persist approval events).

class news.models.ApprovedArticleLog(*args, **kwargs)

Bases: Model

Persists each article-approval event received by the /api/approved/ endpoint. This simulates an external system logging approved content.

Attributes

articleArticle

The approved article being logged.

logged_atdatetime

When the approval event was logged.

notesstr

Optional notes about the approval event.

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

class news.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)

Bases: Model

A news article written by a journalist.

An article is either:

  • An independent article (publisher is NULL, author is the journalist).

  • A publisher article (publisher is set).

Attributes

titlestr

Title of the article.

contentstr

Main content of the article.

sectionstr

Section/category of the article (e.g., politics, sports).

story_imageImageField

Optional image for the article.

weather_locationstr

Location for weather articles.

statusstr

Publication status (draft, pending_review, published).

tagsQuerySet[Tag]

Tags associated with the article.

authorCustomUser

The journalist who authored the article.

publisherPublisher or None

The publisher, if any.

approvedbool

Whether the article is approved for publishing.

approved_byCustomUser or None

The editor who approved the article.

approved_atdatetime or None

When the article was approved.

editor_feedbackstr

Feedback from the editor.

created_atdatetime

When the article was created.

updated_atdatetime

When the article was last updated.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

class Section(*values)

Bases: TextChoices

POLITICS = 'politics'
RELIGION = 'religion'
SOCIAL_MEDIA = 'social_media'
SPORTS = 'sports'
TRENDING = 'trending'
WEATHER = 'weather'
class Status(*values)

Bases: TextChoices

DRAFT = 'draft'
PENDING_REVIEW = 'pending_review'
PUBLISHED = 'published'

class news.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)

Bases: AbstractUser

Extended user model with role-based access.

All users have a ‘role’ field. Reader-specific and journalist-specific fields co-exist on the same table; the application enforces that only the relevant subset is populated (the other set is NULL/empty).

Attributes

rolestr

The user’s role (reader, journalist, or editor).

subscribed_publishersQuerySet[Publisher]

Publishers the reader has subscribed to (readers only).

subscribed_journalistsQuerySet[CustomUser]

Journalists the reader has subscribed to (readers only).

groupsQuerySet[Group]

Groups this user belongs to.

user_permissionsQuerySet[Permission]

Specific permissions for this user.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

save(*args, **kwargs)

Persist the user and enforce role-field consistency.

Reader subscription fields are only meaningful for Reader users. If the user role is Journalist or Editor, these many-to-many relations are cleared to satisfy the project rule.

Parameters

*argstuple

Positional arguments passed to the parent save method.

**kwargsdict

Keyword arguments passed to the parent save method.

class news.models.Newsletter(*args, **kwargs)

Bases: Model

A curated collection of articles assembled by a journalist or editor.

Attributes

titlestr

Title of the newsletter.

descriptionstr

Description of the newsletter.

created_atdatetime

When the newsletter was created.

authorCustomUser

The journalist or editor who created the newsletter.

Articles included in the newsletter.

news.permissions module

Custom DRF permission classes for role-based API access.

class news.permissions.IsEditor

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access only to authenticated users with the Editor role.

has_permission(request, view)

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class news.permissions.IsEditorOrJournalist

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access to editors and journalists.

has_permission(request, view)

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class news.permissions.IsJournalist

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access only to authenticated users with the Journalist role.

has_permission(request, view)

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class news.permissions.IsReader

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access only to authenticated users with the Reader role.

has_permission(request, view)

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

news.serializers module

serializers.py - Django REST Framework serializers for the News Application.

Provides serialization for Article and ApprovedArticleLog models, used by the /api/approved/ endpoint.

class news.serializers.ApprovedArticleLogSerializer(*args, **kwargs)

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for ApprovedArticleLog.

On creation (POST), only ‘article’ and optional ‘notes’ are required. The nested article detail is returned on read (GET).

Fields

idint

Log entry ID.

articleint

ID of the approved article.

article_detailArticleSerializer

Nested article details (read-only).

logged_atdatetime

When the approval event was logged.

notesstr

Optional notes about the approval event.

class Meta

Bases: object

fields = ['id', 'article', 'article_detail', 'logged_at', 'notes']
model

alias of ApprovedArticleLog

read_only_fields = ['id', 'logged_at', 'article_detail']
validate_article(article)

Ensure the referenced article is actually approved before logging.

Parameters

articleArticle

The article to validate.

Returns

Article

The validated article instance.

Raises

serializers.ValidationError

If the article is not approved.

class news.serializers.ArticleSerializer(*args, **kwargs)

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for Article model used by the article REST API.

Fields

idint

Article ID.

titlestr

Title of the article.

contentstr

Main content of the article.

authorint

ID of the author (journalist).

author_usernamestr

Username of the author (read-only).

publisherint or None

ID of the publisher (optional).

publisher_namestr or None

Name of the publisher (read-only).

sectionstr

Section/category of the article.

section_displaystr

Human-readable section name (read-only).

statusstr

Status of the article.

status_displaystr

Human-readable status (read-only).

weather_locationstr

Location for weather articles.

story_imageImage

Optional image for the article.

tag_idslist[int]

IDs of tags associated with the article.

tag_nameslist[str]

Names of tags (read-only).

approvedbool

Whether the article is approved.

editor_feedbackstr

Feedback from the editor.

created_atdatetime

When the article was created.

updated_atdatetime

When the article was last updated.

approved_atdatetime or None

When the article was approved.

news.signals module

signals.py - Django signals for the News Application.

Uses post_save on Article to detect when an article transitions from unapproved to approved, then:

  1. Emails all relevant subscribers.

  2. POSTs the approval event to the internal /api/approved/ endpoint.

news.signals.on_article_post_save(sender, instance, created, **kwargs)

Triggered every time an Article is saved.

We only act when:

  • The article is NOT newly created, AND

  • The ‘approved’ field has just been set to True.

The pre-save state is tracked via a ‘_previously_approved’ attribute set in the Article model’s save() method override below. If that attribute is absent (first-time saves), we compare against False as the safe default.

news.signals.on_user_post_save(sender, instance, created, **kwargs)

Ensure each user belongs to the Django group that matches their role.

Skips group sync when the save only touches fields other than ‘role’

news.tests module

tests.py - Unit tests for the News Application.

Test coverage:

  1. Model tests (CustomUser, Publisher, Article, Newsletter, ApprovedArticleLog)

  2. Role-group assignment via assign_role_group()

  3. View access-control tests (authentication & authorisation)

  4. Article approval workflow (signal triggers, email, API log)

  5. REST API endpoint tests (/api/approved/)

  6. Subscription view tests

class news.tests.AdditionalResourcesRestAPITest(methodName='runTest')

Bases: TestCase

Tests for /api/users, /api/newsletters, and /api/publishers endpoints.

auth(token)
setUp()

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_authenticated_user_can_list_publishers_and_newsletters()

Authenticated users can list publisher/newsletter resources.

test_newsletter_author_can_update()

Newsletter author can update newsletter details via API.

test_newsletter_cannot_include_unapproved_articles()

Newsletter serializer rejects unapproved article IDs.

test_newsletter_create_by_journalist()

Journalist can create newsletter via API and serializer writes article_ids.

test_newsletter_create_forbidden_for_reader()

Reader cannot create newsletter via API.

test_publisher_create_editor_only()

Only editors can create publishers via API.

test_users_endpoint_editor_only()

Only editors can list users through /api/users/.

class news.tests.ApprovedArticleAPITest(methodName='runTest')

Bases: TestCase

Tests for the ApprovedArticleLog REST API endpoint.

These tests simulate calling the third-party integration endpoint and verify that approval events are correctly persisted.

setUp()

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_get_approved_logs_unauthenticated()

Any user (including unauthenticated) can GET the list of approval logs.

test_get_response_contains_article_detail()

GET /api/approved/ returns nested article_detail in each log entry.

test_post_creates_log_entry()

An authenticated POST to /api/approved/ creates an ApprovedArticleLog.

test_post_unapproved_article_returns_400()

POSTing an unapproved article ID returns HTTP 400 (validation error).

test_post_unauthenticated_allowed_for_internal_signal()

Unauthenticated POST to /api/approved/ is allowed for internal signal-triggered integration simulation.

class news.tests.ArticleAccessControlTest(methodName='runTest')

Bases: TestCase

Tests that ensure access control is enforced on article views.

setUp()

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_editor_can_view_pending_queue()

Editors can access the pending articles queue.

test_editor_home_status_filter_shows_only_selected_status()

Editor can filter home feed by article workflow status.

test_journalist_can_create_article()

Journalists can access the article creation view.

test_journalist_can_save_article_as_draft()

Journalists can explicitly save a draft instead of submitting review.

test_journalist_can_upload_story_image()

Journalist can upload a story image when creating an article.

test_pending_queue_status_filter_can_show_drafts()

Editor status filter on pending queue can switch from pending to drafts.

test_reader_cannot_create_article()

Readers are redirected from the article creation view.

test_reader_cannot_view_pending_article()

Readers are redirected when trying to view an unapproved article.

test_reader_cannot_view_pending_queue()

Readers are redirected from the pending articles queue.

test_reader_home_hides_workflow_status_controls()

Readers do not see workflow status filters and status query is ignored.

test_reader_home_is_paginated()

Reader home feed paginates article listings.

test_reader_home_search_filters_visible_articles()

Reader search narrows the approved feed by keyword.

test_unauthenticated_user_redirected_from_create()

An unauthenticated user is redirected to the login page.

test_weather_section_auto_generates_content(mock_weather)

Weather section article prepends auto-generated weather summary.

test_weather_section_manual_when_auto_toggle_off(mock_weather)

Weather section remains manual when auto weather toggle is disabled.

class news.tests.ArticleApprovalTest(methodName='runTest')

Bases: TestCase

Tests for the article approval view and signal workflow.

setUp()

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_approval_can_store_editor_feedback()

Editor feedback entered during approval is persisted on article.

test_approval_marks_article_approved(mock_post, mock_notify)

POSTing to the approve view sets article.approved=True and stores the approving editor.

test_editor_can_access_approve_page()

Editors can load the article approval confirmation page.

test_editor_group_member_can_approve()

Users assigned to Editor group can approve even if role is not editor.

test_get_article_subscribers_includes_publisher_subscribers()

get_article_subscribers includes readers subscribed to the publisher when the article has a publisher.

test_get_article_subscribers_returns_reader_emails()

get_article_subscribers returns the reader’s email when they are subscribed to the article’s journalist.

test_non_editor_cannot_approve()

Journalists and readers cannot access the approve view.

class news.tests.ArticleModelTest(methodName='runTest')

Bases: TestCase

Tests for the Article model.

setUp()

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_approval_sets_approved_flag()

Setting approved=True and saving marks the article approved.

test_article_defaults_to_unapproved()

Newly created articles are unapproved by default.

test_article_str_returns_title()

Article __str__ returns its title.

test_clean_allows_publisher_without_author()

Model clean should not crash when author is unset but publisher is set.

test_previously_approved_attribute_on_save()

The save() override stores _previously_approved to help signals detect when approval status changes.

Bases: TestCase

Tests for token-auth article endpoints and role-based authorization.

auth(token)

setUp()

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_api_login_failure()

POST /api/login/ rejects invalid credentials.

test_api_login_success()

POST /api/login/ returns a token for valid credentials.

test_editor_can_approve_article()

Editor can approve article via PUT /api/articles/<id>/approve/.

test_editor_can_delete_article()

Editor can delete any article via API.

test_editor_group_user_can_approve_article()

User in Editor group can approve article via API.

test_get_articles_can_filter_by_section()

GET /api/articles/?section=sports returns only sports approved articles.

test_get_articles_requires_authentication()

Unauthenticated GET /api/articles/ is denied.

test_get_articles_returns_only_approved()

GET /api/articles/ returns approved articles only.

test_get_single_article_for_reader()

Reader can retrieve approved article details.

test_get_subscribed_articles_can_filter_by_section()

Subscribed feed supports optional section filtering.

test_journalist_can_create_article()

Journalist can create article via POST /api/articles/.

test_journalist_can_update_own_article()

Journalist can update their own article.

test_journalist_cannot_approve_article()

Journalist cannot approve article via API.

test_journalist_cannot_update_other_article()

Journalist cannot update another journalist’s article.

test_non_reader_cannot_get_subscribed_articles()

Journalists cannot call reader-specific subscribed feed endpoint.

test_reader_cannot_create_article()

Reader is forbidden from POST /api/articles/.

test_reader_cannot_delete_article()

Reader cannot delete article via API.

test_reader_cannot_get_unapproved_article()

Reader receives 404 for unapproved article detail endpoint.

test_reader_get_subscribed_articles_only()

Reader gets only approved content from subscribed publishers/journalists.

news.tests.make_article(author, title='Test Article', approved=False, publisher=None, section='trending', status_value=None)

Create and return an Article.

news.tests.make_user(username, role=Role.READER, password='testpass123')

Create and return a CustomUser with the given role.

news.urls module

urls.py - URL routing for the ‘news’ application.

Defines all URL patterns for the news app, including authentication, articles, newsletters, publishers, subscriptions, and API endpoints. All URL patterns are included directly by the project’s urls.py.

news.utils module

utils.py - Helper utilities for the News Application.

Provides:

  • assign_role_group(): assigns a user to the correct Django group/permissions based on their role.

  • get_article_subscribers(): collects email addresses of all subscribers for a given article (readers subscribed to the journalist or publisher).

  • notify_subscribers(): sends a notification email to all subscribers.

  • post_to_api(): sends a POST request to /api/approved/ to log an approval.

news.utils.assign_role_group(user)

Add a user to the Django group that matches their role.

Also removes stale group memberships from previous roles.

Parameters

userUser

The user to assign to the correct group.

news.utils.can_act_as_editor(user)

Check if the user can act as an editor (by role or group).

Parameters

userUser

The user to check.

Returns

bool

True if the user is an editor, False otherwise.

news.utils.can_act_as_journalist(user)

Check if the user can act as a journalist (by role or group).

Parameters

userUser

The user to check.

Returns

bool

True if the user is a journalist, False otherwise.

news.utils.can_act_as_reader(user)

Check if the user can act as a reader (by role or group).

Parameters

userUser

The user to check.

Returns

bool

True if the user is a reader, False otherwise.

news.utils.generate_weather_story(location='Harare')

Build an automatic weather story snippet for the given location.

Uses Open-Meteo geocoding + forecast endpoints (no API key required).

Parameters

locationstr, optional

The location (city name) for which to generate the weather story. Defaults to ‘Harare’.

Returns

str or None

Human-readable weather summary, or None if data cannot be fetched.

Raises

requests.RequestException

If there is a network or API error (caught internally, returns None).

news.utils.get_article_subscribers(article)

Return a list of unique email addresses for all subscribers of the given article (based on journalist subscription or publisher subscription).

Parameters

articleArticle

The approved article whose subscribers should be notified.

Returns

list[str]

Deduplicated list of subscriber email addresses.

news.utils.has_group(user, group_name)

Return True when the authenticated user belongs to a Django group.

Parameters

userUser

The user to check.

group_namestr

The group name to check for membership.

Returns

bool

True if the user is in the group, False otherwise.

news.utils.notify_subscribers(article)

Send a notification email to all subscribers of the given article.

Parameters

articleArticle

The newly approved article to notify subscribers about.

Raises

Exception

If sending email fails (logged, not raised).

news.utils.post_to_approved_api(article, request=None)

Send a POST request to the internal /api/approved/ endpoint to log the newly approved article.

Parameters

articleArticle

The approved article to be logged.

requestHttpRequest, optional

Used to build the absolute URL. When called from a signal (no request context), falls back to localhost.

Raises

requests.RequestException

If the POST request fails (logged, not raised).

news.utils.setup_groups()

Create (or update) the three role-based groups with correct permissions.

Call this once during app startup via AppConfig.ready() or via a management command / migration.

news.views module

views.py - View functions for the News Application.

Organised into sections:

  1. Authentication (login, logout, register)

  2. Home / article listing

  3. Article CRUD + approval

  4. Newsletter CRUD

  5. Publisher management

  6. Subscription management

  7. REST API views (ApprovedArticleLog)

class news.views.ApprovedArticleLogListCreateView(**kwargs)

Bases: APIView

GET /api/approved/ – list all approval log entries. POST /api/approved/ – log a newly approved article.

This endpoint is called internally by the post_save signal whenever an editor approves an article, simulating a third-party integration.

get(request)

Return a list of all approval log entries.

get_permissions()

Instantiates and returns the list of permissions that this view requires.

post(request)

Persist a new approval event sent by the internal signal.

class news.views.ArticleApproveAPIView(**kwargs)

Bases: APIView

PUT /api/articles/<id>/approve/ -> approve article (editors only).

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>, <class 'news.permissions.IsEditor'>]
put(request, pk)
class news.views.ArticleDetailAPIView(**kwargs)

Bases: APIView

GET /api/articles/<id>/ -> retrieve a single article. PUT /api/articles/<id>/ -> update article (editors/journalists). DELETE /api/articles/<id>/ -> delete article (editors/journalists).

delete(request, pk)
get(request, pk)
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>]
put(request, pk)

Bases: APIView

GET /api/articles/ -> list approved articles for authenticated users. POST /api/articles/ -> create article (journalists only).

post(request)

class news.views.SubscribedArticleListAPIView(**kwargs)

Bases: APIView

GET /api/articles/subscribed/ -> approved articles from reader subscriptions.

get(request)
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>, <class 'news.permissions.IsReader'>]
class news.views.UserDetailAPIView(**kwargs)

Bases: APIView

GET /api/users/<id>/ -> retrieve a single user (editors only).

get(request, pk)
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>, <class 'news.permissions.IsEditor'>]
class news.views.UserListAPIView(**kwargs)

Bases: APIView

GET /api/users/ -> list all users (editors only).

get(request)
permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.IsAuthenticated'>, <class 'news.permissions.IsEditor'>]
news.views.article_approve_view(request, pk)

Allow an editor (by role or group) to approve a submitted article.

Only users with role=’editor’ or in the ‘Editor’ group may access this view. On POST, marks the article as approved, notifies subscribers, and logs the event.

Parameters

requestHttpRequest

The HTTP request object.

pkint

Primary key of the article.

Returns

HttpResponse

The rendered approval page or redirect on success/error.

news.views.article_create_view(request)

Allow journalists to submit a new article for editorial review.

Parameters

requestHttpRequest

The HTTP request object.

Returns

HttpResponse

The rendered article form or redirect on success/permission error.

news.views.article_delete_view(request, pk)

Allow the article author or an editor to delete an article.

Parameters

requestHttpRequest

The HTTP request object.

pkint

Primary key of the article.

Returns

HttpResponse

The rendered confirmation page or redirect on success/error.

news.views.article_detail_view(request, pk)

Show a single article. Non-approved articles are hidden from readers.

Parameters

requestHttpRequest

The HTTP request object.

pkint

Primary key of the article.

Returns

HttpResponse

The rendered article detail page or redirect if not permitted.

news.views.article_edit_view(request, pk)

Allow the original journalist or an editor to edit an article.

Parameters

requestHttpRequest

The HTTP request object.

pkint

Primary key of the article.

Returns

HttpResponse

The rendered article form or redirect on success/permission error.

news.views.home_view(request)

Render the home page with article listings based on user role.

  • Unauthenticated/reader: shows all approved articles.

  • Journalist: shows their own articles (any status) + all approved.

  • Editor: shows all articles (approved and pending).

Parameters

requestHttpRequest

The HTTP request object.

Returns

HttpResponse

The rendered home page with context.

news.views.login_view(request)

Authenticate and log in a user.

Parameters

requestHttpRequest

The HTTP request object.

Returns

HttpResponse

The rendered login page or a redirect to home on success.

news.views.logout_view(request)

Log out the current user.

Parameters

requestHttpRequest

The HTTP request object.

Returns

HttpResponse

Redirect to the login page after logout.
